text: Why should foam be banked off a bulkhead when extinguishing an oil fire?

options.text: To speed up chemical stabilization loops of active foam bubbles
To prevent agitation of the oil and spreading the fire
To maximize the coverage range of standard delivery equipment
To avoid cooling structural steel components unevenly

explanation: When applying foam to an oil fire, banking it off a bulkhead prevents the direct force of the foam stream from agitating the oil surface. Agitation can spread the burning oil and potentially worsen the fire, hindering extinguishment.
